other_observers: Joey Kellner, Steve Larson, Dick Schottler, Paul Differding, Allison Kondler; I have no recollection as to who initially found the bird, but it was re-found by each of us at some point as it moved along the road in the canopy. I determined that no one had submitted a record and decided to rectify the situation.
description: Solitary Vireo type.   Blue-gray head with bright white spectacles.   Bright white chest and belly with bright yellow flanks and lower sides.  Sharp demarcation between gray of cheeks and the clean white throat.  Obvious contrast between the blue-gray head and the olive-green back.Behaviors: Primarily working the tops of deciduous trees and spontaneously singing.Call: Song matched eastern "Solitary Vireo" song.Plumage: Adult
